hormones.definition: Chemicals made by the body that control how you feel and how your body works.
hormones.example: Hormones change a lot during pregnancy.

pregnancy.definition: The time when a baby grows inside a mother's body.
pregnancy.example: During pregnancy, your emotions may feel stronger than usual.

multiplied.definition: Made much larger or stronger; increased many times.
multiplied.example: It can feel as if every emotion has been multiplied by ten.

olympics.definition: A big international sports competition held every four years.
olympics.example: She was watching the Winter Olympics on television.

skating.definition: Moving on ice wearing special shoes with blades.
skating.example: Speed skating is a very exciting sport to watch.

rooting.definition: Supporting and hoping for someone to win.
rooting.example: She was rooting for her favorite athlete.

crashed.example: The skater crashed out of the race after falling.

burst.definition: To suddenly start doing something with strong emotion.
burst.example: She burst into tears watching the skater fall.

emotional.example: I am not usually this emotional, but pregnancy changed that.

exaggerated.definition: Made to seem bigger or more important than it really is.
exaggerated.example: Your feelings may seem exaggerated but they still matter.

significant.example: What you feel charged up about may be significant for you.

metaphor.definition: When something is described as something else to show a deeper meaning.
metaphor.example: The skater's crash was a metaphor for her own fears about things going wrong.

anxieties.definition: Feelings of worry and fear about what might happen.
anxieties.example: The story touched on her own anxieties about becoming a mother.

sportswoman.example: The sportswoman did her best but still fell.

release.definition: The act of letting go of strong emotions.
release.example: Crying about the skater gave her some much-needed emotional release.

model.definition: A good example that you can follow or learn from.
model.example: The skater getting up again is a good model for you.

exaggeration.definition: Making something seem bigger or stronger than it is.
exaggeration.example: The strong feelings are just an exaggeration of what you already feel.

attuned.definition: Very sensitive and aware of something.
attuned.example: Having highly attuned feelings helps you respond to your baby.

responsive.definition: Quick to react and pay attention to needs.
responsive.example: Being more responsive to your baby is a benefit of heightened emotions.

charged.definition: Filled with strong emotion or energy.
charged.example: She felt charged up about things that normally would not bother her.
